name: Kamakura
description: Kamakura , officially Kamakura City , is a city of Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan. It is located in the Kantō region on the island of Honshu. The city has an estimated population of 172,929 and a population density of 4,359 people per km2 over the total area of 39.67 km2 (15.32 sq mi). Kamakura was designated as a city on 3 November 1939.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Samurai Heritage Tour
itineraries.description: The classic Kamakura route focusing on the most famous shrines and the Great Buddha.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kamakura Station and walk up the iconic Wakamiya Oji approach.
Visit Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, the most important Shinto shrine in Kamakura.
Street food and souvenir shopping along Komachi-dori street.
Take the Enoden train to Hase Station. Visit the giant bronze Great Buddha.
Explore Hase-dera temple and its beautiful hillside gardens and ocean views.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Bamboo Groves & Ocean Breezes
itineraries.description: Avoid the main crowds by exploring eastern Kamakura and the nearby island of Enoshima.

steps.activity: Take a bus to Hokoku-ji Temple. Enjoy matcha tea in the serene bamboo grove.
Lunch at a quiet local cafe in the Jomyoji area.
Ride the Enoden train all the way to Enoshima.
Walk across the bridge to Enoshima Island and explore the shrine and caves.
Watch the sunset over Mt. Fuji from the Enoshima Sea Candle.

notes: Wear comfortable walking shoes. Enoden line can be very crowded.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Kamakura via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons
